macro_segments.title: Introduction and Review of Borrower Exemptions (Shoel Petur)

micro_segments.title: Opening remarks and transition from previous discussion on ptur of shoel
Two explanations for why meita machmat melachah exempts the shoel
Case of Rav ruling on the broken axe and halachic conclusion
Rav Pappa's case: borrowed bucket that broke and burden of proof on shoel
Discussion of why shoel cannot exempt himself with shvua and Ritva's opinion

macro_segments.title: The Cat Case and Extreme Circumstances (Meita Machmat Melachah)

micro_segments.title: Case of borrowed cat killed by mice as example of meita machmat melachah
Rav Ashi's question and Rav Mordechai's answer citing the saying of Avimi from Rava
Alternative version: cat overstuffed itself with mice and died
Rashi's interpretation of the phrase gavra denashei katluha as overindulgence

macro_segments.title: Borrowing Without Liability: Employment Loophole and its Closure

micro_segments.title: Rava's case: borrowing something while simultaneously hiring the owner to work
Clever response: owner requires work to be completed before lending object

macro_segments.title: Service Professionals and the Presumption of Employment (Bealav Imo)

micro_segments.title: Rava's ruling: school teachers, gardeners, butchers, and bloodletters as perpetual public employees
Debate between Rashi and Rif on whether employment is communal or individual

macro_segments.title: School Teacher Hiring Students and Control Over Curriculum

micro_segments.title: Students claim to have hired teacher; Rava reverses: students are hired to learn from teacher

macro_segments.title: Service Professionals and the Presumption of Employment (Bealav Imo) (continued)

micro_segments.title: Modern application: internet providers, rabbis, and the difficulty of determining employment status

macro_segments.title: School Teacher Hiring Students and Control Over Curriculum (continued)

micro_segments.title: Teacher's authority: can choose which masechta to teach on regular days
Okimta: fixed curriculum during challa periods obligates students differently
Application to Daf Yomi and modern contractual arrangements

macro_segments.title: Loading Donkey Case: Determining if Owner Was Helping or Supervising

micro_segments.title: Mar bar Rav Chanina's case: donkey dies during loading; Rabba rules chayav
Rabbis object: this is pshia b'baalim which should exempt; investigation reveals owner was supervising, not helping
Shift to alternative explanation: package was stolen, not negligence (gneiva b'baalim)

macro_segments.title: Mishnah: Borrowed Cow on Different Days (Shoel vs Socher Liability)

micro_segments.title: Mishnah cases: borrowing and renting on different half-days or full days; cow dies
Lender claims death occurred during borrowing; borrower says 'I don't know'; lender wins (bari v'shema)
Renter claims death occurred during rental; owner says 'I don't know'; renter exempt
Direct contradiction (bari v'bari): renter must swear death occurred during rental
Both say 'I don't know' (shema v'shema): they split the liability (yachloku)

macro_segments.title: Gemara Analysis: Reconciling Mishnah with Rav Nachman's Opinion on Uncertain Claims

micro_segments.title: Question: does Mishnah contradict Rav Nachman that in 'I don't know' cases one pays only if shvua obligation exists
Resolution: gilgul shvua—the socher is liable for another shvua with the same lender, so shvua kicks in here
Second step: since socher's claim is shema (not bari), he cannot take the shvua and must pay
Parallel case of modeh b'miktzat illustrating forced shvua and subsequent inability to take it

bealim metaplim bineveilah (owner keeps broken object)

donkey loading case (Mar bar Rav Chanina)

mitoch sh'eino yachol lishava meshallem (must pay because cannot swear)
